name: Coast Guard Sector Upper Mississippi River
tagline: The Coast Guard keeping the upper Mississippi and Missouri river system moving, based in St. Louis.
_note: Coast Guard Sector Upper Mississippi River (St. Louis, MO). CONUS full template (BAH MHA MO161 St. Louis; RentCast/SchoolDigger work). CG uses same BAH tables. Inland-rivers sector covering a huge stretch of the upper Mississippi and Missouri rivers (commercial traffic, SAR, aids to navigation, pollution response). Medical: CG clinic + TRICARE network. Facts verified from official sources (uscg.mil, TRICARE, CGX, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). Missouri is TRICARE East. Affordable metro. Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx), off-base ZIP 63129 (south St. Louis County).

data.Role: Coast Guard inland-rivers hub for the upper Mississippi and Missouri rivers
data.Mission: Commercial river traffic, search and rescue, aids to navigation, and pollution response
data.Setting: Affordable Midwest metro on the river
data.Note: An 'inland' Coast Guard tour, far from the ocean

source.label: Coast Guard Sector Upper Mississippi River (official)

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Coast Guard Sector Upper Mississippi River, based in St. Louis, keeps a vast inland river system moving, the upper Mississippi and Missouri rivers, handling commercial barge traffic, aids to navigation, search and rescue, and pollution response.
It's in the St. Louis metro, an affordable Midwest city on the river with four real seasons.

source.label: Coast Guard Sector Upper Mississippi River (official)

data.subtitle: St. Louis is an affordable metro with a wide range of suburbs. The main choice is which area fits your commute and schools, with Illinois suburbs across the river too.
intro: St. Louis's cost of living is low, so BAH stretches well. Coast Guard housing is limited, so most families rent or buy across the metro.
Where you live mostly comes down to commute and schools across St. Louis County, St. Charles County, and the Metro East (Illinois) across the river.

recommendations.advice: St. Louis County and St. Charles County offer a wide range of homes and well-regarded schools.
The Illinois Metro East is an affordable option a short drive over the river.
St. Louis is affordable, so BAH tends to go far and many families buy.
Forest Park, the Arch, and a strong food scene are part of the appeal.

source.label: Coast Guard Sector Upper Mississippi River (official)

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Visitors without a DoD/CG ID need to check in at the visitor control point.
Requirements change, so confirm the current details on the official Coast Guard site before you go.

source.label: Coast Guard Sector Upper Mississippi River (official)

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: Coast Guard family housing in the area is limited, so most families rent or buy across the St. Louis metro.
Eligibility and availability vary, so start with the Coast Guard housing office for current options and waitlists.

source.label: Coast Guard Sector Upper Mississippi River (official)
DoD 2026 BAH Rates, MHA MO161 (St. Louis) — Coast Guard uses the same BAH tables

sections.title: Schools
data: There is no DoDEA district here; children attend the local civilian public schools. St. Louis County and St. Charles County districts (and Illinois Metro East districts) serve military families depending on where you live.
Your assigned schools depend on your address, so confirm the district and campus for any neighborhood you are considering.

data.Clinic: Coast Guard clinic, Sector Upper Mississippi River
data.Care: On-base clinic care for the assigned population; most family care is via the TRICARE network and St. Louis-area hospitals (a major medical region)

data.Commissary: Limited locally; nearest DoD commissaries are at Scott AFB (Illinois) and Fort Leonard Wood (south-central MO)

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves, and St. Louis's affordable market makes buying a real option for many families.
rent: Your tour is short (about two years or less) or your orders feel uncertain.
You want time to learn the metro before committing.
You'd rather not gamble on resale timing when you PCS out.
Your BAH comfortably covers rent in the areas you'd consider.
buy: You expect to be here three or more years.
You want to use your VA loan and build equity instead of paying rent.
The affordable St. Louis market fits your budget for a home you like.
You've found a suburb and commute you're confident about.
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer, but St. Louis's affordability makes buying worth a look for longer tours. Run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, weigh your tour length, and talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics. Knocking these out early makes everything else easier.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book lodging or a short-term rental before you arrive. You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days while you house-hunt.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Check housing
steps.text: Get the housing office's read on St. Louis County, St. Charles, and the Metro East before you sign anything.

steps.title: Register your vehicle and sort access
steps.text: Handle base access and Missouri (or Illinois) vehicle registration, and learn the visitor process for guests. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact the gaining district (St. Louis County, St. Charles, or Metro East) early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE enrollment and pick a network provider; St. Louis is a major medical region.

data.q: What is Sector Upper Mississippi River?
data.a: It's a Coast Guard inland-rivers hub, based in St. Louis, covering the upper Mississippi and Missouri rivers: barge traffic, aids to navigation, search and rescue, and pollution response.

data.q: Do Coast Guard families get BAH?
data.a: Yes, the same BAH tables as the rest of the military. St. Louis is MHA MO161.
Across the St. Louis metro, including St. Charles County and the Illinois Metro East. CG housing is limited, so most rent or buy.

data.q: Is it really a Coast Guard base?
data.a: Yes. The Coast Guard's inland-rivers mission keeps the nation's river-barge system safe, far from any coast.
